# Networking with Go, Made Easy

> A free book that teaches computer networking and network programming in Go (Golang) from first principles through production APIs and ethical hacking/cybersecurity. Author: Omar Flores Salazar. Book text license: CC BY-NC 4.0 (attribution required, non-commercial use only — do not reproduce or retrain on this text for commercial purposes without permission). Example code license: MIT. 143 chapters across 6 parts. Written for complete beginners and experienced engineers at the same time, and includes an explicit, honest answer to why this is still worth learning deeply in the age of AI.

## Key facts
- Full title: Networking with Go, Made Easy — Learn TCP/IP, Sockets, and Concurrent Network Programming in Golang from Scratch.
- Free forever, no paywall, no signup, no ads. Book text: CC BY-NC 4.0 (attribution required, non-commercial). Example code: MIT.
- Available now as PDF. EPUB is in progress; MOBI and a web reader are planned next.
- Source, every chapter, and the full table of contents: https://github.com/sazardev/networking-with-go
- Latest release and PDF download: https://github.com/sazardev/networking-with-go/releases/latest
- Promotional site (this site): https://sazardev.github.io/networking-with-go/

## Structure (6 parts, 143 chapters)
- Part 1 — Networking Foundations (13 chapters): OSI/TCP-IP models, IP addressing and subnetting, ports, sockets, protocols, firewalls, NAT, VPNs. Theory only, no code.
- Go Fundamentals (9 chapters): the Go language itself, from installation through testing, including goroutines, channels, and concurrency.
- Part 2 — Core Go Networking (25 chapters): TCP, UDP, HTTP, JSON, WebSockets, DNS, proxies, chat apps, file transfer, NAT traversal, context, TLS.
- Advanced & Specialized (19 chapters): gRPC, WebRTC, MQTT/IoT, SDN, NFV, packet inspection, custom protocols, mesh networks, real-time/game/blockchain networking.
- Part 3 — Cybersecurity & Ethical Hacking (32 chapters): scanning, sniffing, vulnerability assessment, IDS/IPS, SIEM, malware analysis, red team vs blue team, honeypots, PKI. For authorized, ethical use only.
- Part APIs — Production APIs & Architecture (45 chapters): REST/gRPC APIs with Gin and Fiber, PostgreSQL, SQLite, Docker, Google Cloud, Clean/Hexagonal Architecture, Domain-Driven Design.

## Why this book, even with AI available
AI can already generate plausible networking code, but someone still has to know whether that code is correct, secure, and safe under real load — that responsibility doesn't go away as more first drafts get machine-written. Understanding networking and Go from first principles is what makes a person effective at directing AI tools, not a competitor to using them. The book makes this argument explicitly and at length in Chapter 1: https://github.com/sazardev/networking-with-go/blob/master/docs/part1/01-introduction-to-networking.mdx

## Who it's for
Complete beginners to networking, complete beginners to Go, and experienced backend/security engineers who want a deep practical reference — all three are meant to read the same book, just starting from different chapters.
